response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with 15+ years of broad experience and recent exposure to AI-agent tooling (MCP frameworks and agentic workflows), but he falls substantially short of the director-level IC profile this role demands. The position requires recognized AI expertise with demonstrated products or platforms at scale, strategic customer advisory capacity, and deep cloud platform knowledge — none of which are evidenced in the resume at the required depth. His AI involvement is limited to infrastructure tooling in his current role, his career arc is individual contributor Staff-level throughout, and there is no indication of the technical thought leadership or executive-facing advisory work central to this position.
strengths: MIT Computer Science degree combined with 15+ years of diverse engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq, demonstrating active engagement with agentic AI patterns
Entrepreneurial track record through GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, founded 2016) plus multi-stack versatility including cloud-adjacent tooling such as AWS CDK and Kubernetes
concerns: No director-level or principal engineering experience; the entire career arc is individual contributor up to Staff level with no evidence of strategic leadership, org influence, or executive advisory capacity

AI expertise is shallow and recent — limited to tooling and infrastructure work, with no ML/AI academic credentials, no recognized thought leadership, and no demonstrated AI products or platforms built at scale

Cloud platform depth is insufficient for the role; AWS CDK usage at a single company and a self-managed Kubernetes deployment do not constitute the enterprise cloud expertise required to advise strategic customers on cloud and AI adoption
